changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "We're a Mixed Grade?"

TextENGNew: ``...Hey, Rena—you're the same age as me, right?``
``Yeah, we are.@k We have the same zodiac sign, too.``
Come on now, it'd be downright odd to be the same age but have a different zodiac sign...
``That's not true, you know. Different birthdays can mean different zodiac signs even at the same age!``
``Huh? Oh, right.@k Mion, you're actually pretty sharp!``
``Ahahaha. So what month were you born, Keiichi-kun?@k Rena's July!``
changeReason: New translation uses Rena's characteristic third-person self-reference ('Rena's July') matching JPN レナは７月, where original used first person ('I'm July').

TextENGNew: Rena puffed out her chest proudly.
...Hey now, what's that supposed to mean?
She's not planning to lord it over me if her birthday comes just a little bit earlier than mine, is she...
``...Heh! Give it up.@k You can't beat me in a birthday contest!!@k ...If you want, we can wager on who's older—with a penalty game for the loser!``
``Huh? What?!@k Why is that?@k Why is that?!``
Rena flustered at being challenged out of nowhere.
...The degree of her panic told me she had no confidence in winning.
That said... throwing her into a tizzy over something as trivial as a birthday was just too fun to resist.
``That means you're an April birthday, Kei-chan?``
``That's exactly right! Better luck next time, Rena!@k I've already got a good head start on you in age.``
``Oh! Is that so!@k Then you and Mii-chan are the same age!``
``Well, just for a few months~! That gap's going to widen again real soon!``
Mion laughed through her nose.

...Hey now, that's nothing to strut about...
Then again, neither is what I was doing. (Wry laugh.)
``...Come to think of it, Mion—you're actually in the grade above me, aren't you.``
``If younger girls are more your type, I could be an underclassman starting today~?``
``Mii-chan's saying things I don't quite follow...``
One glance at Rena's crimson face told me she understood perfectly well...
``So Satoko and Rika-chan are underclassmen then. ...More than that—aren't they the sort of age difference that should put them in a different school entirely?``
``Ke... Keiichi-kun, I think your taste in girls runs just a little too young... I think that...``
You're the one saying things I don't follow, Rena...
I grabbed her head and gave it a rough, playful scruffing.
``I've been meaning to ask—why does this school mix different grade levels in the same class?``
``Not enough classrooms. Nothing to be done about it.@k We're subletting from the forestry office, after all~.``
...Now that she put it that way.@k I'd been thinking our school was odd for a while.
The schoolyard's all gravel, there are rooms with nothing to do with classes, and strange construction equipment sits parked out front.
``Why are you subletting in the first place? What happened to the actual school?``
``There was one standing here from before the war, apparently... It fell into disrepair.@k So they shut it down.``
It must have been quite a dignified, atmospheric building.
``So after that, students were supposed to attend the school in town—but that's quite a long way, right?``
``Which school is that?``
``You know if you go past the station in Okinomiya, turn at the hospital, and go straight toward the pediatrics clinic—there's a school right across from it?``
``Wh-Whaaaat?!?! Th-That's so far...!``
I had no idea of the geography myself, but Rena's reaction made it clear it was quite a distance.
``So that's the situation.@k The kids who didn't want to commute to Okinomiya rented space from the forestry office and set up this makeshift school here.``
``About half the children in Hinamizawa, I think. Quite a few of them ride their bikes here early in the morning.``
``Well, attending a ramshackle place like this makes it pretty tough to get into any serious secondary school, I imagine.``
``That's not true, Mii-chan. If you apply yourself, you can study anywhere.``
``That's right, that's exactly right! Rena and I are in total agreement on this!``
``Yeah, that's right. That's right!@k Let's do our best!``
``By all means, good luck. This old man will be cheering you on from the sidelines.``
``Not just us—you too, Mion! Aren't you supposed to be studying for entrance exams?!@k With grades like yours, your future is looking pretty bleak!``
``Fine, fine. If I end up on the street, I'll just land a permanent position as Kei-chan's wife and let him feed me☆.``
changeReason: New translation correctly captures the JPN 永久就職 euphemism for marriage ('permanent position as Kei-chan's wife'), where original missed this joke and just said 'mooch off Kei-chan.'

TextENGNew: ``Wh-wh-what is a permanent position?! What does that mean?!``
changeReason: New translation correctly has Rena asking about the term '永久就職' (permanent position), matching the JPN. Original changed her question to be about 'mooching,' which was a mistranslation of the previous line.

TextENGNew: ``Hey! You three, keep it down!@k Study hall is supposed to be quiet!``
All three of us got a sharp scolding from the teacher.
Terribly sorry about that...
Satoko watched and tittered to herself.
I stuck out my tongue at her in response.
...Mion was right, there's no question.@k This school has nothing to do with academic advancement.
But in its place, there were plenty of things here you'd never find at any other school.
